Description: The "Log First Call" button is displayed twice on the Lead Details page—once in the top action bar and again inside the SLA notification card. This creates confusion regarding the primary action and results in UI redundancy.
Expected Result: The button should appear only once in the most appropriate location.
Actual Result: Two identical "Log First Call" buttons are visible on the same screen.

Description: Large unused spaces are present between sections and around content, reducing information visibility and requiring unnecessary scrolling.
Expected Result: Screen space should be utilized efficiently with balanced spacing.
Actual Result: Significant empty space is visible across the page.

Description: The lead status "New" is shown in multiple locations (SLA banner, lead header, and lead information section), causing redundant information display.
Expected Result: Lead status should be displayed once prominently.
Actual Result: Same status appears multiple times on the page.

Description: The SLA timer ("16 min remaining") is displayed at the bottom of the information section and is easy to miss.
Expected Result: SLA countdown should be highly visible near the top of the page.
Actual Result: SLA information lacks visual emphasis.

Description: Tabs display "Follow Ups (0)" and "Activities (0)" without any empty-state message or action guidance.
Expected Result: Users should see a meaningful empty-state message and CTA.
Actual Result: Empty tabs provide no guidance to users.

Description: There is no visual indication of the lead lifecycle (New → Contacted → Qualified → Deal Created). Users cannot quickly identify the lead stage progression.
Expected Result: A stage/progress tracker should be displayed.
Actual Result: No lead progression indicator is available.

Issue Title: Mark Lost Available for New Lead
Description: The "Mark Lost" action is available immediately for a newly created lead without requiring any interaction or contact attempt.
Expected Result: System should require at least one activity or confirmation before marking a lead as lost.
Actual Result: Users can mark a new lead as lost directly.

Issue Title: Orange Brand Color Changes to Brown in Dark Mode
Description: The primary orange brand color used for buttons, badges, and highlights appears brown when Dark Mode is enabled. This causes inconsistency with the application's design system and weakens the visual prominence of primary actions.
Expected Result: The brand orange color should remain visually consistent and accessible across both Light and Dark themes.
Actual Result: In Dark Mode, the orange color is rendered as a brown shade, affecting buttons, status indicators, and highlighted elements.

Issue Title: Calendar Icon Not Visible in Dark Mode
Description: The calendar icon used in the Follow Ups section is not visible or has very low visibility when Dark Mode is enabled. Users may not recognize the date selection functionality.
Expected Result: The calendar icon should be clearly visible and maintain sufficient contrast in both Light and Dark modes.
Actual Result: The calendar icon is hidden, faded, or blends into the background in Dark Mode.

Description: The orange brand color is rendered as a brown shade in Dark Mode.
Expected Result: Brand orange color should remain consistent and clearly visible in both Light and Dark themes.
Actual Result: Orange-colored cards and containers appear brown in Dark Mode.

Issue Title: Start Qualification Button Has Poor Visibility
Description: The "Start Qualification" action has very low contrast and appears disabled.
Expected Result: The button should have sufficient contrast and be clearly identifiable as a clickable action.
Actual Result: The button text blends into the background and looks inactive.

Issue Title: Low Contrast Between Cards and Background
Description: Lead information cards blend into the page background.
Expected Result: Cards should be visually distinguishable from the page background.
Actual Result: Cards and background use similar dark shades, reducing section visibility.

Description: Overview, Follow Ups, and Activities tabs are difficult to read in Dark Mode.
Expected Result: Tab labels should maintain adequate contrast and readability.
Actual Result: Tab labels appear faded and less noticeable.

Description: Different UI elements use inconsistent colors in Dark Mode.
Expected Result: Theme colors should follow a consistent design system across all components.
Actual Result: Buttons, badges, and cards use mismatched color shades.

Description: The same action button appears both in the top header and inside the status banner.
Expected Result: The action should be displayed only once in the most relevant location.
Actual Result: "Add Another Follow-Up" is displayed twice on the same screen.

Issue Title: Poor Visibility of Disabled "Mark as SQL" Button
Description: The disabled "Mark as SQL" button has very low contrast in Dark Mode and appears almost invisible.
Expected Result: Disabled buttons should remain clearly visible while indicating they are inactive.
Actual Result: Button text and icon are difficult to see against the background.

Description: Placeholder values such as "e.g. 500000" and "e.g. Within 1 month" are difficult to read in Dark Mode.
Expected Result: Placeholder text should maintain accessibility contrast standards.
Actual Result: Placeholder text appears faded and hard to read.

Description: "Required for SQL" is displayed multiple times after every field, creating visual clutter.
Expected Result: Validation requirements should be displayed once or in a concise format.
Actual Result: The same message is repeated multiple times.

Description: Budget and Timeline fields are marked mandatory (*), but Decision Maker and Genuine Lead checkboxes are also mandatory without a clear mandatory indicator.
Expected Result: All mandatory fields should have consistent indicators.
Actual Result: Checkboxes appear optional until the warning message is read.

Description: The close (X) icon in the top-right corner has poor visibility against the dark header background.
Expected Result: Close icon should be clearly visible and easy to identify.
Actual Result: Close icon appears faded and difficult to notice.

Issue Title: Brown Tint Applied to Modal Background
Description: Modal header and footer use a brown shade instead of the application's primary orange theme color.
Expected Result: Theme colors should remain consistent in Dark Mode.
Actual Result: Brown color is displayed instead of brand orange.

Description: The warning message at the bottom has weak contrast between text and background.
Expected Result: Warning messages should be easily readable and visually distinct.
Actual Result: Text visibility is reduced in Dark Mode.

Issue Title: Unnecessary Actions Displayed After SQL Qualification
Description: After successfully qualifying a lead as SQL, the popup displays multiple actions: "Convert Lead", "View SQL Leads", and "Add More Activities". These actions may confuse users and create unnecessary decision points.
Expected Result: After SQL qualification, the popup should display only the relevant action "View SQL Leads" and a close option.
Actual Result: The popup displays additional actions such as "Convert Lead" and "Add More Activities".

Description: The "Convert Lead" action appears both in the top action bar and inside the SQL status banner.
Expected Result: Convert Lead should be displayed once as the primary action.
Actual Result: Same action is visible in two locations.

Description: The "Add SQL Activity" option has low contrast and does not appear as a primary clickable action.
Expected Result: Action links should be clearly visible and identifiable.
Actual Result: The action blends into the banner background.

Description: The "View SQL Leads" action is difficult to notice because of insufficient contrast in Dark Mode.
Expected Result: Secondary actions should remain readable in Dark Mode.
Actual Result: Text appears faded.

Description: Lead is already qualified as SQL, but Activities tab still shows 0 activities. Qualification itself should generate an activity log entry.
Expected Result: Qualification activity should be recorded and reflected in the Activities count.
Actual Result: Activities tab displays 0 after SQL qualification.

Issue Title: Follow-Up Count Not Updated Based on SQL Actions
Description: After qualification and progression, follow-up/activity history should reflect the lead journey.
Expected Result: Timeline should show all qualification actions.
Actual Result: Lead progression is not visible in activity history.
